title: 街角的早餐摊
coverQuote: The breakfast stall on the corner opens at 6am. By 9am it is gone. If you do not know it is there, you miss it.

sentences.en: There is a breakfast stall on the corner near my apartment. It opens at 6am.
sentences.zh: 我公寓附近的街角有一个早餐摊。早上六点开门。

sentences.en: The woman who runs it makes jianbing. One hand cracks the egg, the other spreads the batter. She has been doing this for sixteen years.
sentences.zh: 摊主是个做煎饼的女人。一只手打蛋，另一只手抹面糊。她已经这样做了十六年。

sentences.en: By 9am the stall is gone. The cart folds up and rolls away. If you do not know it is there, you miss it.
sentences.zh: 早上九点，摊子就消失了。推车折叠起来推走了。如果你不知道它在哪，你就会错过。

sentences.en: I go every Saturday. She knows my order: one egg, no cilantro, extra chili.
sentences.zh: 我每周六都去。她知道我要什么：一个蛋，不要香菜，多辣。

sentences.en: This Saturday the stall is not there. The corner is just a corner again. I stand there for a minute with my hands in my pockets.
sentences.zh: 这个周六摊子没来。街角又变回了普通的街角。我手插在口袋里在那儿站了一分钟。

title: 地下通道的吉他手
coverQuote: The man in the underground passage plays the same three songs every night. I know the order now. First the slow one, then the fast one, then the sad one.

sentences.en: Every night around 9pm, a man with a guitar sits down in the underground passage between Line 1 and Line 2.
sentences.zh: 每天晚上九点左右，一个带着吉他的男人在一号线和二号线之间的地下通道坐下来。

sentences.en: He plays the same three songs every night. I know the order now. First the slow one. Then the fast one. Then the sad one.
sentences.zh: 他每晚弹同样的三首歌。我现在知道顺序了。先是慢的那首。然后是快的那首。然后是伤心的那首。

sentences.en: His guitar case is open in front of him. There are a few coins inside. A five-yuan note. A pack of tissues someone left.
sentences.zh: 他的吉他盒在面前打开着。里面有几枚硬币。一张五块钱。一包谁留下的纸巾。

sentences.en: I drop a coin in as I walk past. He does not look up. He is in the middle of the sad song. His fingers move on the strings like they belong there.
sentences.zh: 我经过时丢了一个硬币进去。他没抬头。他正在弹伤心的那首。他的手指在弦上移动，好像它们本来就该在那。

sentences.en: I slow down as I walk past. I always do. The sad song follows me up the stairs and into the night.
sentences.zh: 我经过时刻意放慢了脚步。每次都是。伤心的歌跟着我上了楼梯，进入夜色中。

title: 城建围挡后的路
coverQuote: The city is always under construction. The familiar path becomes unfamiliar overnight. A new wall. A detour sign.

sentences.en: I walked the same route to work for two years. I knew every crack in the pavement.
sentences.zh: 我走同一条路去上班走了两年。我认识人行道上的每一道裂缝。

sentences.en: This morning there was a blue construction wall where the old bakery used to be. The bakery closed six months ago. Now even the building is gone.
sentences.zh: 今天早上，老面包店原来的地方竖起了一道蓝色施工围挡。面包店六个月前就关了。现在连那栋楼都没了。

sentences.en: The city is always under construction. The familiar becomes unfamiliar overnight. A new wall. A detour sign. You learn to adapt.
sentences.zh: 这座城市永远在施工中。熟悉的东西一夜之间变陌生。一道新墙。一块绕行指示牌。你学会了适应。

sentences.en: I had to walk an extra block to get around it. The detour took me past a noodle shop I had never noticed before.
sentences.zh: 我不得不绕一个街区才能过去。绕路经过了一家我从来注意到的面馆。

sentences.en: Maybe I will try the noodle shop tomorrow. The old bakery is not coming back. But the noodle shop smells good.
sentences.zh: 也许明天我会去那家面馆试试。老面包店回不来了。但面馆闻起来很香。

title: 公园长椅上的十分钟
coverQuote: I sit on a park bench and watch a leaf fall. It takes longer than you would think.

sentences.en: I found a bench near the pond. It was empty.
sentences.zh: 我在池塘边找到一张长椅。是空的。

sentences.en: I sat down. No phone. No book. Just sitting.
sentences.zh: 我坐下来。没带手机。没带书。就是坐着。

sentences.en: A leaf fell from the tree above. It took a long time to reach the ground.
sentences.zh: 一片叶子从头顶的树上落下来。它用了很久才到地面。

sentences.en: An old man walked past with a small dog. The dog stopped to look at me.
sentences.zh: 一个老人带着一只小狗走过去。小狗停下来看了看我。

sentences.en: I stayed for ten minutes. That is longer than I have done nothing in a while.
sentences.zh: 我待了十分钟。比我很久以来什么都不做的时间都长。

title: 公园里喂猫的人
coverQuote: Every evening at 6pm, a woman comes to the park with a paper bag. The cats know her.

sentences.en: I see her every evening. She arrives at 6pm with a crumpled paper bag.
sentences.zh: 我每晚都看到她。她六点到，拿着一个皱皱的纸袋。

sentences.en: Three cats appear from behind the bushes. A gray one. An orange one. A black one with a white paw.
sentences.zh: 三只猫从灌木丛后面出来。一只灰的。一只橘的。一只黑猫有一只白爪子。

sentences.en: She pours dry food into a small plastic bowl. The cats eat in a neat row, like commas on a page.
sentences.zh: 她把猫粮倒进一个小塑料碗里。三只猫排成一排吃，像纸上的逗号。

sentences.en: She does not pet them. She just watches with her hands in her pockets.
sentences.zh: 她不摸它们。她只是手插在兜里看着。

sentences.en: When the food is gone, the cats leave one by one. The woman picks up the bowl and walks away.
sentences.zh: 吃完了，猫一只一只走了。女人拿起碗，走开了。

sentences.en: I have never spoken to her. I do not know her name. But at 6pm, we are both in the park.
sentences.zh: 我从来没跟她说过话。我不知道她的名字。但下午六点，我们都在公园里。

title: 深夜房间听播客
coverQuote: I lie in the dark with one earbud in, listening to someone talk about history. Outside, a distant siren.

sentences.en: I put in one earbud and press play. A voice starts talking about ancient Rome.
sentences.zh: 我塞了一只耳机，按下播放。一个声音开始讲古罗马。

sentences.en: I do not care about ancient Rome. But the voice is calm and the room is dark.
sentences.zh: 我不在乎古罗马。但那个声音很平静，房间很暗。

sentences.en: I lie on my back with one earbud in. Half listening. Half drifting.
sentences.zh: 我仰面躺着，戴着一只耳机。半听半飘。

sentences.en: Outside, a distant siren passes. The voice keeps talking about aqueducts.
sentences.zh: 外面，远处传来一阵警笛声。那个声音继续讲着引水渠。

sentences.en: The episode ends. The next one auto-plays. I do not turn it off.
sentences.zh: 这集结束了。下一集自动播放。我没关。

sentences.en: At some point I fall asleep. In the morning the earbud is tangled in my hair.
sentences.zh: 不知什么时候我睡着了。早上耳机缠在头发里。

title: 车窗外的雨夜
coverQuote: The backseat of a taxi on a rainy night is a small dry world. Outside, the city dissolves.

sentences.en: I get into a taxi just as the rain gets heavier. Good timing for once.
sentences.zh: 我刚坐进出租车雨就下大了。难得的好时机。

sentences.en: The backseat of a taxi on a rainy night is a small dry world. Outside, the city dissolves into streaks of color.
sentences.zh: 雨夜里出租车后座是一个小小的干燥世界。外面，城市溶化成彩色的条纹。

sentences.en: The driver hums along to an old song on the radio. His voice is slightly off-key. I do not mind.
sentences.zh: 司机跟着广播里一首老歌哼着。他有点跑调。我不介意。

sentences.en: We pass a brightly lit hot pot restaurant. Steam on the windows. People laughing inside. A frame of warmth in the wet dark.
sentences.zh: 我们经过一家灯火通明的火锅店。窗户上都是蒸汽。里面的人在笑。湿冷的黑夜里一框暖意。

sentences.en: The meter ticks up. The wipers swing. The driver hums. I lean my head against the window and watch.
sentences.zh: 计价器在跳。雨刷在摆。司机在哼歌。我靠着车窗看着。

title: 凌晨三点醒来喝水
coverQuote: Waking up at 3am to drink water is a small ritual. You stand in the dark kitchen and for a moment, the world is just you and the glass.

sentences.en: I wake up at 3:14am. The red numbers on the clock confirm it.
sentences.zh: 凌晨三点十四分我醒了。钟上的红色数字确认了这一点。

sentences.en: My mouth is dry. I slide out of bed and walk to the kitchen without turning on lights.
sentences.zh: 嘴里很干。我滑下床，摸黑走到厨房。

sentences.en: I pour water from the pitcher. The glug-glug sound is loud in the silent kitchen.
sentences.zh: 我从凉水壶里倒水。咕嘟咕嘟的声音在安静的厨房里很响。

sentences.en: I stand by the sink and drink. For a moment the world is just me and the glass.
sentences.zh: 我站在水槽边喝水。有那么一会儿世界只有我和这个杯子。

sentences.en: I rinse the glass, leave it on the rack, and go back to bed. The whole thing took two minutes.
sentences.zh: 我冲了杯子，放在沥水架上，回到床上。整个过程花了两分钟。

title: 周日黄昏的街道
coverQuote: Sunday evening streets have a specific kind of quiet. The weekend is over but Monday has not started yet.

sentences.en: I go for a walk on Sunday evening. The streets are quieter than usual.
sentences.zh: 周日傍晚我出去散步。街道比平时安静。

sentences.en: Sunday evening has a specific kind of quiet. The weekend is over but Monday has not started yet. A gap.
sentences.zh: 周日的傍晚有一种特定的安静。周末结束了，周一还没开始。一个缝隙。

sentences.en: A father teaches his daughter to ride a bike in the empty parking lot. She wobbles. He cheers.
sentences.zh: 一个爸爸在空停车场教女儿骑车。她摇摇晃晃的。他在喊加油。

sentences.en: The convenience store lights flicker on. The streetlights follow a few minutes later.
sentences.zh: 便利店的灯亮了。几分钟后路灯也跟着亮了。

sentences.en: I walk until the sky is completely dark. Then I turn around and walk home.
sentences.zh: 我一直走到天完全黑了。然后转身走回家。

title: 地铁站台上的学生
coverQuote: A girl in school uniform sits on the platform bench, highlighting her textbook in neon yellow. The train comes. She does not look up.

sentences.en: The platform is crowded at 5pm. Students in uniforms. Office workers with loosened ties.
sentences.zh: 下午五点的站台挤满了人。穿校服的学生。松了领带的上班族。

sentences.en: A girl sits on the bench, bent over a textbook. She highlights a line in neon yellow.
sentences.zh: 一个女孩坐在长椅上，埋头看课本。她用荧光黄划了一行。

sentences.en: The train arrives with a rush of wind. People push forward. She does not look up.
sentences.zh: 列车带着一阵风进站。人们往前挤。她没有抬头。

sentences.en: She turns a page. Highlights another line. The train doors close and leave without her.
sentences.zh: 她翻了一页。又划了一行。列车门关了，没带她就走了。

sentences.en: The next train will come in four minutes. She has four more minutes of studying.
sentences.zh: 下一趟车四分钟后到。她还有四分钟可以学习。
